description: This image displays a grain boundary characterization map of Fe-8wt%Al low-density ferritic steel after hot deformation, with a 500 μm scale bar provided. Black and red lines are assigned to high-angle grain boundaries (θ≥15°) and low-angle subgrain boundaries (2°≤θ<15°) respectively, while the two opposite arrows mark the shear deformation direction during hot torsion tests that simulate hot rolling conditions. The observed microstructure features initial ferrite grains elongated along the shear direction, with subgrains and newly nucleated recrystallized grains formed near original grain boundaries, reflecting dynamic recrystallization evolution. This microstructure evidence supports the core conclusion that dynamic recrystallization mechanisms (continuous or discontinuous) can be regulated by adjusting hot deformation parameters, and discontinuous dynamic recrystallization that yields weak random texture is beneficial to reduce roping defects in subsequent deep drawing of the steel sheets.
